name: Squats
description: Fundamental lower body exercise targeting quads, glutes, and hamstrings.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out
Brace core and push hips back as you lower down
Lower until thighs are parallel to the floor or deeper
Keep chest up and heels flat on the floor
Drive through heels to return to standing — 8–12 reps

name: Lunges
description: Unilateral leg exercise for quad, glute, and balance development.
instructions: Stand with feet together, hands on hips or holding weights
Step one foot forward and lower back knee toward the floor
Keep front knee above ankle and torso upright
Push off front foot to return to starting position
Alternate legs for 10–12 reps per side

name: ATG Split Squats
description: Deep range split squat reaching full hip and knee flexion for maximum leg development.
instructions: Step one foot forward into a lunge stance
Lower the back knee all the way to the floor
Lean the front shin forward to maximize depth
Drive through front foot to return to start
Perform 8–10 reps per side

name: Split Squats
description: Stationary lunge that isolates each leg individually for strength and balance.
instructions: Stand in a split stance with one foot forward, one back
Lower straight down by bending both knees
Keep front knee tracking over the toes
Drive through front heel to return to start
Perform 8–12 reps per side

name: RDL's
description: Hip hinge movement targeting the hamstrings and glutes with a slight knee bend.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding weights in front of thighs
Push hips back and lower the weights along your legs
Stop when you feel a strong hamstring stretch
Keep back flat and a slight bend in the knees
Drive hips forward to return — 8–12 reps

name: Single Leg RDL's
description: Single-leg hip hinge that challenges balance while targeting hamstrings and glutes.
instructions: Stand on one leg with a slight bend in the knee
Hinge forward at the hip, extending the free leg behind for balance
Lower weight toward the floor while keeping back flat
Return to standing by driving through the heel
Perform 8–10 reps per side

name: Tibialis Raises
description: Isolation exercise for the tibialis anterior (shin muscle) to prevent shin splints.
instructions: Stand with heels on a step or against a wall
Lift the front of your feet (toes toward shins) as high as possible
Hold briefly at the top, then lower with control
Keep legs straight throughout the movement
Perform 15–20 reps

name: Leg Curls
description: Machine or band exercise that isolates the hamstrings.
instructions: Lie face down on a leg curl machine with ankles under the pad
Curl both legs up toward your glutes
Squeeze hamstrings at the top
Lower with control back to start
Perform 10–15 reps

name: Leg Extension
description: Quad isolation exercise performed on a machine.
instructions: Sit on a leg extension machine with ankles behind the pad
Extend both legs until straight, contracting the quads
Hold briefly at the top
Lower with control back to start
Perform 10–15 reps

name: Calf Raises
description: Isolation exercise for the calves to build lower leg strength and size.
instructions: Stand with the balls of your feet on an elevated surface
Lower heels as far as possible for a full stretch
Drive up onto your toes as high as possible
Hold at the top for a brief squeeze
Perform 15–20 reps

name: Romanian Deadlift
description: Hip hinge movement targeting the hamstrings and lower back.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, barbell or dumbbells in front
Hinge at hips keeping back flat and bar close to legs
Lower until a strong stretch is felt in the hamstrings
Drive hips forward to return to standing
Perform 8–12 reps

name: Single Leg Step Ups
description: Unilateral step-up onto a box to develop quad and glute strength.
instructions: Stand in front of a sturdy box or step
Place one foot fully on the box
Drive through that heel to lift your body onto the box
Step back down with control
Perform 10–12 reps per side

name: Lat Pull Downs
description: Cable exercise that builds upper back width by training the latissimus dorsi.
instructions: Sit at a lat pulldown machine, grip bar wider than shoulder-width
Lean slightly back and pull the bar down to upper chest level
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the bottom
Extend arms slowly to return to start
Perform 10–12 reps

name: Rows Tief
description: Low horizontal row targeting the middle and lower back.
instructions: Sit at a cable row station, grip the handle with both hands
Keep back straight and pull handle toward your lower abdomen
Drive elbows back and squeeze shoulder blades together at the end
Extend arms with control to return to start
Perform 10–12 reps

name: Rows Hoch
description: High horizontal row targeting the upper back and rear deltoids.
instructions: Stand or sit at a cable station set at upper chest height
Pull the handle toward your upper chest or chin
Keep elbows flared out to shoulder height
Squeeze upper back at the end of the pull
Perform 10–12 reps

name: Reverse Butterflies
description: Rear delt isolation targeting the back of the shoulders and upper back.
instructions: Sit at a pec deck machine facing inward, or hinge forward holding dumbbells
Start with arms forward, slight bend at elbows
Pull arms back in a wide arc out to the sides
Squeeze rear delts and upper back at the end
Return slowly to start — 12–15 reps

name: Back Extension
description: Lower back extension exercise that strengthens the erector spinae and glutes.
instructions: Position yourself face-down on a back extension bench
Start with torso hanging down at about 90°
Raise torso by extending the lower back until body is straight
Squeeze glutes at the top, do not hyperextend
Lower with control — 12–15 reps

name: Flies
description: Chest isolation exercise using a wide arc to stretch and squeeze the pecs.
instructions: Lie on a bench holding dumbbells above chest, palms facing each other
Lower arms out to the sides in a wide arc with a slight elbow bend
Feel the stretch across the chest at the bottom
Bring arms back together in the same arc, squeezing chest
Perform 12–15 reps

name: Incline Flies Low Chest
description: Incline dumbbell fly targeting the lower portion of the chest.
instructions: Set bench to a slight incline (15–30°)
Hold dumbbells above chest with a slight elbow bend
Lower arms out to the sides in a wide arc
Focus on feeling the stretch in the lower chest
Bring arms back together overhead — 12–15 reps

name: Decline Chest Press
description: Pressing movement on a decline bench targeting the lower chest.
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with feet secured
Hold dumbbells or barbell at chest level, elbows at 45°
Press the weight upward until arms are extended
Lower with control to chest level
Perform 8–12 reps

name: Dumbbell Chest Press
description: Classic pressing movement targeting the chest, shoulders, and triceps.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench holding a dumbbell in each hand at chest level
Press both dumbbells upward until arms are fully extended
Keep a slight bend in elbows at the top
Lower with control, feeling the chest stretch at the bottom
Perform 8–12 reps

name: Bankdrücken
description: Barbell bench press — the fundamental compound chest exercise.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with eyes below the bar
Grip bar slightly wider than shoulder-width
Unrack and lower bar to mid-chest with control
Press bar back up to starting position
Perform 5–8 reps — use a spotter with heavy weight

name: Gewichtsscheibe Press
description: Chest press with a weight plate to build grip strength and chest power.
instructions: Lie on a bench holding a weight plate at chest with both hands on the edges
Press the plate directly upward until arms are extended
Keep hands flat against the plate throughout
Lower with control back to chest level
Perform 12–15 reps

name: Overhead Chest Curls
description: Overhead dumbbell movement that targets the upper chest.
instructions: Lie on a bench with dumbbells extended overhead
Lower the weights in a curling arc toward the top of your head
Keep arms parallel, feel the upper chest engage
Curl weights back to overhead position
Perform 12–15 reps

name: Lateral Raises
description: Shoulder isolation targeting the medial deltoid for shoulder width.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at sides, slight bend in elbows
Raise both arms to the sides until they reach shoulder height
Keep wrists neutral and avoid shrugging
Lower with control back to sides
Perform 12–15 reps

name: Shoulder Press
description: Overhead pressing movement for overall shoulder and upper body strength.
instructions: Hold dumbbells or barbell at shoulder height, palms facing forward
Brace core and press weights straight overhead
Fully extend arms at the top
Lower with control back to shoulder level
Perform 8–12 reps

name: Shoulder Front Raises
description: Anterior deltoid isolation using a forward raise motion.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at sides, palms facing back
Raise both arms straight forward to shoulder height
Keep a slight bend in the elbows and avoid swinging
Lower with control
Perform 12–15 reps

name: Shoulder Pulls
description: Horizontal pulling movement targeting rear delts and upper back.
instructions: Hold a resistance band or cable at shoulder height
Pull toward your face, spreading hands apart
Drive elbows back and squeeze rear delts
Return slowly to starting position
Perform 12–15 reps

name: Reverse Fly
description: Bent-over fly that isolates the rear deltoid and upper back.
instructions: Stand with a slight forward lean, dumbbells hanging below chest
Raise both arms out to the sides in a wide arc
Keep a slight bend in the elbows and lead with the elbows
Squeeze rear delts at the top
Lower with control — 12–15 reps

name: Shoulder Shrugs
description: Trap isolation exercise to build upper trapezius size and strength.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells or barbell at sides
Shrug both shoulders straight up toward your ears
Hold briefly at the top, fully contracting the traps
Lower with control, fully stretching the traps
Perform 12–15 reps

name: Upright Rows
description: Vertical pull targeting the upper traps and side deltoids.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells or barbell in front of thighs
Pull weight straight up along your body toward the chin
Lead with your elbows, keeping them above the wrists
Do not pull higher than the chin
Lower with control — 10–12 reps

name: Crunches
description: Classic ab exercise that targets the rectus abdominis.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat, hands behind head
Brace core and curl head and shoulders off the floor
Exhale at the top, do not pull on your neck
Lower with control back to start
Perform 15–20 reps

name: Planks
description: Full-body isometric hold that builds core stability and endurance.
instructions: Start on forearms and toes, elbows below shoulders
Keep body in a straight line from head to heels
Brace core, squeeze glutes, breathe steadily
Avoid letting hips sag or rise
Hold for 30–60 seconds

name: Leg Raises
description: Lower ab exercise performed by raising straight legs from lying position.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with legs extended
Place hands under your lower back for support
Raise both legs to 90° keeping lower back pressed down
Lower legs slowly back toward the floor without touching
Perform 12–15 reps

name: Flutter Kicks
description: Alternating leg movement targeting the lower abs and hip flexors.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with legs extended, hands under glutes
Raise both legs a few centimetres off the floor
Rapidly alternate kicking each leg up and down in small motions
Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Perform for 20–30 seconds

name: Side Crunches
description: Oblique-targeting crunch variation performed from a side-lying position.
instructions: Lie on your side with legs stacked
Place top hand behind your head
Crunch sideways, lifting head and shoulder off the floor
Squeeze obliques at the top
Lower with control — 15 reps per side

name: Curls
description: Classic bicep curl for building arm size and elbow flexor strength.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at sides with palms facing forward
Curl both weights toward your shoulders by bending at the elbow
Squeeze biceps at the top
Lower with control back to starting position
Perform 10–12 reps

name: Wrist Curls
description: Wrist flexor exercise to strengthen forearms and improve grip.
instructions: Sit with forearms resting on thighs, palms facing up, holding weights
Lower the weight by extending wrists toward the floor
Curl wrists upward as far as possible
Keep forearms flat on thighs throughout
Perform 15–20 reps

name: Wrist Extension
description: Wrist extensor exercise for forearm balance and injury prevention.
instructions: Sit with forearms on thighs, palms facing down, holding weights
Lower weights by bending wrists toward the floor
Extend wrists upward as far as possible
Keep forearms flat on thighs throughout
Perform 15–20 reps

name: Hammer Curls
description: Neutral-grip curl targeting the brachialis and brachioradialis alongside biceps.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at sides, palms facing inward
Curl both weights toward shoulders with a neutral grip
Keep elbows close to sides and avoid swinging
Lower with control
Perform 10–12 reps

name: Spider Curls
description: Incline bench bicep curl that eliminates momentum for strict isolation.
instructions: Lie chest-down on a 45° incline bench
Let arms hang straight down, holding dumbbells
Curl the weights toward your face, bending only at the elbows
Squeeze biceps at the top
Lower with full extension — 10–12 reps

name: Preacher Curls
description: Bicep curl on a preacher bench for strict form and peak contraction.
instructions: Sit at a preacher bench with upper arms resting on the pad
Hold a barbell or dumbbell with underhand grip
Curl the weight toward your chin without lifting arms off the pad
Squeeze at the top, then lower fully
Perform 10–12 reps

name: Tricep Push Downs
description: Cable exercise for tricep isolation by pushing the attachment downward.
instructions: Stand at a cable machine with rope or bar attachment at head height
Grip with both hands, elbows tucked at sides
Push the attachment down until arms are fully extended
Squeeze triceps at the bottom, then return slowly
Perform 12–15 reps

name: Single Arm Push Downs
description: Unilateral cable push-down to isolate each tricep individually.
instructions: Stand at a cable machine with a single handle attachment
Grip with one hand, elbow tucked at your side
Push down until arm is fully extended
Squeeze tricep at the bottom and return slowly
Perform 12–15 reps per side

name: Single Arm Triceps Extension
description: Overhead single-arm extension for full tricep stretch and contraction.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell in one hand overhead with arm extended
Bend elbow to lower the dumbbell behind your head
Keep upper arm stationary and close to your head
Extend arm back overhead to full extension
Perform 10–12 reps per side

name: Samurai Extensions
description: Two-handed overhead tricep extension for full tricep stretch and contraction.
instructions: Hold a single dumbbell with both hands overhead
Lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending both elbows
Keep elbows close together and pointing forward
Extend arms back overhead to full lockout
Perform 12–15 reps

name: African Curls
description: Bicep curl variation with a rotating wrist motion for full muscle engagement.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at sides, palms facing back
Curl the weight up while rotating wrists outward (supinating)
End with palms facing up at the top of the curl
Lower with control, rotating back to starting position
Perform 10–12 reps
